free_text: On the Slow Neutron Absorption Resonances in Iodine
The iodine used for the absorbers was resublimed
iodine crystals with a minimum purity of 99.985
percent.
BF3 ionization chamber.
The transmission by several thicknesses of
iodine absorbers was measured over the energy
interval from 0.0026 eV to 1000 eV, corresponding to
a time-of-flight interval from 2.2 microsec/m to 1400
microsec/m.
BP
Below 3 eV, the iodine cross section was found to
obey the 1/v absorption law.
page 371.
An attempt was made to match a symmetrical
type Breit-Wigner formula to the observed. data at
the 20.3 eV resonance. The scattering cross section,
found by extrapolating to zero time of flight the
straight line which best fitted the data on a
cross-section vs. time-of-flight graph, is 3.6x10**24
cm2/atom.
No details on sources of uncertainties.
page 362, abstract.
3 m flight path.
The statistical probable errors in the points
are indicated by the lengths of the vertical lines
through the points, or for those points with no
vertical line the probable error is no greater than
the diameter of the point.
